desc:
The Road to the Grey Havens bends south-west here. South of the road are plains
and to the north and east is a group of trees. It's nothing like a forest but it
is so big that you can't see how far it stretches to the north. There are tracks
leading north-east into the trees.
name: Bend in the Road

desc:
The Road to the Grey Havens bend north-east here. The countryside to the south
is pretty uninteresting. Just empty plains and occasionally some vegetation. The
road shows signs of years of use, like tracks of horses and horse-wagons. There
are ruts in the road which makes progress a bit annoying.
name: Bend in the Road

desc:
The Road to the Grey Havens goes east-west here. The road is in a good
condition. To the north is an area with bushes and trees. You hear a humming
coming from the north and you see bees flying around. You notice tracks on the
ground leading northwards. South of the road are plains filled with bushes and
other small plants.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The Road to the Grey Havens goes east-west here. Bushes are to north and south
of the road and further northwest you see a group of trees. The bushes in the
north look like someone is tending them, the ones in the south don't. Also there
is more life in the area north-west of you than in the south.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The Road to the Grey Havens bends south-west here. The road is surrounded by
boring looking plains. There isn't much vegetation growing in the plains just a
few bushes and a tree every mile or so. A broken tent is scattered over the
ground just south of the road.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The Road to the Grey Havens bends south-west here. In the east you can see a a
well-tended path leading into the dense vegetation. In the north the road is
bordered by undergrowth that is too dense to enter. South of the road is the
savanna - a place full of life.
name: Bend in the Road

desc:
The Road to the Grey Havens bends north-east here. In the north-east the road is
bordered by dense vegetation making it impossible to go there. To the south and
west of the road lies the savanna. The road shows signs of years of use but is
still in a quite good condition.
name: Bend in the Road

desc:
The road goes east and west, leading to lands full of mystery and adventure.
Everything around you is quiet and still, and the soft hum in the air gives life
to this place. To the west, you know you will find the legendary Grey Havens, a
place where, it's said, the magic of the past ages can still be found.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The trail gets wider to the east, running through the low hills. Despite the
rich vegetation, not a single green shoot can be spotted on the well-travelled
path. Standing water and mud cover the trail, keeping clear footprints for days.
name: Small Dirt Path

desc:
A narrow yet wet, earthy track suddenly disappears in a debris of wood on the
eastern slope of a large hill. The trail winds its way around the several tiny
trees, keeping a general west-east direction. Unlike the surrounding slopes, the
path is not covered by vegetation and a lot of fresh tracks can be seen on it.
name: Small Dirt Path

desc:
A thick, secluded grove surrounds the narrow passage between two hills. Signs of
life are plentiful, a lot of animal tracks are leading in and out of the forest.
Farther to the west, the view is almost blocked by a man-high wall of bushes.
name: Small Hills

desc:
The high grass, interspersed with occasional trees and bushes, covers the steep
hill slopes to the north and south. A footpath, void of any footprints, was once
much wider but now almost disappears amongst the plants. A huge oak tree stands
a little off the path, giving refreshing shade from the hot summer sun.
name: Path in the Hills

desc:
Rows of tall trees and high bushes surround this barely visible trail that runs
in an west-east direction. Small, white stones are scattered all over the path,
making it difficult to walk. Tracks of some small animals are crossing the
footpath in many places, leading deep into brushwood. A valley opens out wider
to the east.
name: The Small Path

desc:
The dense pine forest covers both hillsides of this wide valley, making it
impossible to leave the trail. The forest thins towards the bottom of the
valley, giving more sunlight to other forms of vegetation. The trail is covered
by a thin layer of earth, with some small stones raised here and there. A small
junction can be seen a few miles to the east.
name: The Small Path

desc:
Two trails meet here forming a three-way passage between the surrounding hill
ridges. Light pine forest covers the steep slopes, the trees growing more
densely westwards. A small but obviously well-travelled path winds between tall
pines going in an west-east direction and branching southwards. To the south,
the trail disappears in the high bushes between the trees, but with a little
effort and patience it would be possible to follow it.
name: The Small Path

desc:
Gently sloping hillsides fade into the small valley, protecting it from the
wind. All kinds of colourful flowers bloom around the winding trail emitting a
strange mixture of scents. Debris of rotten logs and stones rest in a small
hollow near the road; being the last remains of a long abandoned watchtower,
once overlooking the surrounding lands and the road to the Grey Havens.
name: High Grass

desc:
Thick, thorny bushes are scattered over the northern and southern parts of the
ridge, making it impossible for travellers to descend. Standing on top, one
could see surrounding hills, forested valleys and the road to Grey Havens far in
the north.
name: Small Hills

desc:
A ridge of hills continues here, reaching its top height. Only a thin layer of
humus covers the flat rocks, with rare islands of low, dry grass. Large and
rough rocks, mixed with sharp thorn-bushes, block the way down. High grass and
trees cover the lower levels of the northern hillslope, slowly descending into a
beautiful green valley.
name: Small Hills

desc:
Eastwards, the ground slopes down and vegetation on the rare hill plateaus
becomes richer. The grass here is green and some low trees grow around, twisted
by frequent winds. Yellow wildflowers are trying to reach the light from the
soil-filled cracks in the broken granite rock, half-buried into the ground.
name: Small Hills

desc:
Huge rocks and boulders lie all over this wide plateau. Most of them are covered
with mould and moss. Few flowers grow on the poor soil between the stones.
Thick, green bushes surround the plateau, protecting it from the constant wind
coming from the Gulf of Lhun.
name: Small Hills

desc:
As the trail bends from the north to the east, the surrounding forest becomes
more dense. The passage between trees narrows and a few old willows arch their
branches over the path. The sound of flowing water can be heard, but no visible
signs of any water source can be seen. Perhaps there is a spring or a brook
somewhere behind some bushes deep inside the forest.
name: Bend in the Path

desc:
As far as the eye can see, the surrounding hills are covered with all kinds of
bushes. Large, thorny bushes contrast with neighbouring flower-covered shrubs.
Dark, two feet high grass covers the wild animals sneaking around. Water burbles
rom a nearby spring and is quickly absorbed into the fertile soil.
name: Path in Hills

desc:
The thin layer of soil covering the hillsides mainly consists of sand and only a
few varieties of vegetation manage to stay alive there. A small footpath
continues to the west and to the east. Steep hills block passageways to the
south and north.
name: The Small Path

desc:
To the east, the trail ends in a large, mud-filled cavity between the hills.
Water is sipping through the cracks in the soil, slowly turning the valley into
a marsh. Broken remains of a wooden wagon are rotting near the trail, probably
left by some unlucky merchant.
name: End of the Path

desc:
The entire area, save for a few circles of exposed rocks, is overgrown with a
dense, green vegetation which completely obscures the view of anyone having
travelled here. Several twisted poplars have spread their branches over the
tiny, mud-covered trail. Occasionally one may notice furry little animals
lurking between the high bushes.
name: The Small Path

desc:
A soft, rocky slope rises northwards with several streams flowing down to the
valley. Water bends around large boulders; what were once rough and sharp
surfaces are polished and smooth now. Wind and rain have eroded the hillside,
forming a lot of small hollows filled with stagnant water.
name: The Small Path

desc:
As the rocky landscape flattens here, the vegetation surrounding the trail
becomes richer. Despite the wild growth, the path remains clear and easy to
follow. Several tall elm trees loom over the trail, protecting travellers from
frequent winds and the light of the sun.
name: The Small Path

desc:
The farther east one follows the rocky trail, the more open the valley becomes.
The slopes of the ridge to the north are completely overgrown with dense thorny
bushes, making it impossible to climb towards the plateau. A number of low trees
grow scattered between the bushes, challenging them for sunlight.
name: Small Hills

desc:
Two lonely hills rise on either side of the trail, hiding travellers from winds
and giving pleasant shade during the hot summer days. Thick, brown bushes with
rare grass-covered clearings surround the twisting path. The top of a once
mighty ridge of mountains rises to the north.
name: Small Hills

desc:
Green grass and flowers cover the landscape in all directions, slowly meeting a
forest far to the west. A few miles north, a long ridge of barren hills can be
seen. Following a small grass-covered path farther east, a traveller would
encounter a passage between two steep hillsides.
name: Small Hills

desc:
Small hills, covered with a light pine forest, lie before you. The remnants of a
small, wooden hut are rotting just a few yards off the forest trail. Farther to
the north, the landscape is much smoother and the trees are replaced by an ocean
of green grass. To the east, a large dale between two hills opens. A small,
earthy footpath makes its way through the forest towards a ridge of hills
southwards.
name: Forest Hills

desc:
Deep, green grass grows on the steep hillsides, and only occasional low trees
give a home to some rare bird nests. Stalks bend and sway in the wind which
blows softly along the valley.
name: Forest Hills

desc:
High, but rather smooth barren hills mark the borders of this majestic valley. A
small trail runs through the green, sharp-looking shrubbery, and slowly
disappears into an ocean of grass on the plains eastwards. A lone oak tree
dominates the view, visible from anywhere within a few miles.
name: Small Hills

desc:
An ocean of grass covers the wide valley, its slopes smoothed by erosion.
Looking closer, one may notice a lot of tiny animal burrows hidden by
vegetation. A lone oak tree breaks the monotonous horizon line a few miles
westwards.
name: Small Hills

desc:
Wide, fertile meadows stretch all over this broad valley. Rich, green grass
shifts slowly as a wind gently moves through it. A grove of elm trees can be
spotted not too far to the west. Eastward the soil muddier and low bushes with
yellow flowers grow there in abundance.
name: Small Hills

desc:
Two smooth hills, one to the north and a second to the south, share this valley
as their baseline. Small clusters of trees are spread around, casting shadows
upon the weary travellers on sunny days. A rapid stream flows down the
hillslope, falling into a calm lake.
name: Small Hills

desc:
The lack of water has made the surrounding lands arid and deserted, only a few
clusters of thin, yellow grass grow in this dusty soil. A rich and fertile
valley can be spotted to the west, stretched between two hills. A weary
traveller may find rest there.
name: The Small Path

desc:
A vast ocean of green grass lies here, undisturbed by mankind for years. Several
tall oaks form a small shady grove, hiding countless bird nests in their dense
treetops. The plains meet an impenetrable pine forest a few miles to the west.
name: The Small Path

desc:
The dense and gloomy forest threathens to swallow everyone passing by. Damp and
mildewy air, coming from the rotten debris, makes breathing difficult. However
signs of wild life can be spotted everywhere: gnats and mosquitoes buzz around
and some small animals run from one burrow to another.
name: The Small Path

desc:
Short pale stalks of grass sprout out of the cracks in the narrow forest trail.
Rotten logs block the path in many places, forcing a slower advance through the
shadowy forest. Thick, green grass grows in the shade of ancient pines covering
fresh tracks of forest inhabitants.
name: Forest Hills

desc:
The top of the hill is covered by small, sharp rocks and several smooth, granite
stones are spread alongside the western slope. Only the rare pale- yellow
sprouts of some flowers managed to make their way through the rocks. A tiny
trail bends around the stones and follows the hillside westwards.
name: Small Hills

desc:
The ridge slopes gently down towards the forest to the west and up towards the
mighty hilltop eastwards. Only rare islands of dry yellow grass break the
monotony of the grey, rocky landscape. Large, round stones are scattered around,
making it difficult to travel mounted.
name: Small Hills

desc:
To the west, a steep forest-covered climb leads down to a small plain between
the set of hills. Light green grass interspersed with some red-white mountain
flowers covers the rare layers of poor soil between the sharp rocks. Going
eastwards, the slope climbs steeply to its peak.
name: Forest Hills

desc:
A steep, forest-covered slope rises quickly towards the large hill in the east.
The soil here is wet and rich, giving life to the various forms of forest flora.
name: Forest Hills

desc:
High green grass forms a fluttering carpet, covering uneven plains. The
vegetation shifts slowly as the wind grabs hold of it. To the east the plains
continue, while to the south a steep, impassable hill-slope blocks the way. Far
to the west, a grassy footpath enters a light pine forest.
name: A Path through the Forest

desc:
The small, grassy trail winds along the passage between two hills, avoiding
large, mossy boulders which lie scattered around. A fallen oak tree blocks the
trail, but one would have little problem going around it or even simply jumping
over it. Small shoots of green grass haphazardly cover the barren northern
hillslope, while the southern one has no visible signs of vegetation at all.
name: The Small Path

desc:
As the trail makes a sharp bend around a large boulder, it runs west through the
valley and towards a small T-type junction a couple of miles to the south. Sharp
pieces of red granite are lying amongst the sturdy bushes on the sides of the
path, forming a natural wall.
name: The Small Path

desc:
Rotten branches and smelly humus litter the floor of this cramped animal den,
situated between two huge evergreen bushes. Green moss covers the huge stone
which blocks the passage to the west. Tracks of some large predators cover the
ground and several large bones are lying here and there.
name: Forest Bear Den

desc:
This is where the rich, grass-covered meadow meets the outskirts of the dense
and shadowy forest. The grass almost disappears and the forest grows sparse. A
large rock rises six feet over the ground being a silent witness.
name: Small Hills

desc:
Walking through the plains, one may stumble upon a small, abandoned plantation
overgrown with grass. Long furrows are hidden beneath the grass, making crossing
this meadow slow or even dangerous when mounted. A partly broken fence surrounds
the meadow, but it is easy to avoid or simply jump over it.
name: The Small Path

desc:
Thick, dark-green grass flourishes around, giving no chance to any other
vegetation. Only a few scattered bushes manage to break through the dense cover
of grass, tangled by the wind. The grasslands and meadows seem to stretch in
every direction with barely visible footpaths criss-crossing and running towards
the horizon. The outskirts of a gloomy forest can be seen a few miles northward.
name: Small Hills

desc:
As the soil becomes more rich and fertile, grass and flowers slowly take over
the small, thorny bushes that dominate the lands westwards. To the east the
endless grasslands continue while far in the north the dark forest stands tall
at the base of massive hills.
name: Small Hills

desc:
Grass covers the land as far as the eye can see. Several hills rise north and
south while flat green plains stretch towards the horizon to the east and west.
A huge, crude rock rises over a small hill a few miles to the east, serving as a
landmark to travellers.
name: Path in the Hills

desc:
A line of small white stones marks the borders of this obviously well-travelled
path between two smooth grass-covered hills. Endless plains are stretching all
the way to the horizon eastwards, but after a few miles, the road splits, giving
the traveller a hard choice between the northern and southern directions.
name: The Small Path

desc:
Small holes, barely visible in short yellow grass, dot the waterless plains
along the path. Several withered oaks are spread along the arid, dusty
landscape. A rough half-rotten sign made of wood marks the junction in the
trail.
name: High Grass

desc:
A grove of tall birch trees breaks the monotony of the surrounding plains. Few
strange, green bushes, covered with bright yellow flowers, are spread amongst
the trees. Water from the nearby spring is supporting the wild life and making
the surrounding lands moist and fertile.
name: The Small Path

desc:
Whether one looks to the west or to the south, the surrounding grasslands seem
to be endless. The area seems to be a neverending sea of fluttering pale-green
grass, nothing else is visible for miles. Earth slowly rises to the north
gradually turning into the smooth hill with a barren yellow hilltop. Among the
rare low bushes birds have made their nests.
name: The Small Path

desc:
A small but rather clear path continues to the east and south. Wild vegetation
is slowly taking over the trail; bushes are spreading their branches over the
path, almost completely obscuring the view. A few more years and travelling
through these lands might be difficult at best.
name: Small Hills

desc:
A little stream springs out of the pile of rocks directly to the north, feeding
the fertile plains around here. A grove of mature willow trees surround the
spring: their heavy, twisted branches dance and wave in the wind. Forested hills
stretch all over the horizon to the northwest.
name: Small Hills

desc:
The trail abruptly disappears in the thick wall of high bushes to the west.
Pawmarks of some large animal lead to a split between two large bushes. Dense
canopy and twisted branches stretch all around, forming a barrier which blocks
out sunlight.
name: Forest Hills

desc:
The tall, dark grass dominates the landscape in western and northern directions,
completely overtaking any other forms of vegetation and slowing the progress of
unmounted travellers. A ridge of hills rises high to the southwest.
name: Small Hills

desc:
High evergreen bushes cover the low hillside, but the hills to the east are
getting much higher and vegetation there becomes more scarce. Another major
range of the Tower Hills lies far to the west of here, a vast ocean of grass
separating it from these hills.
name: Small Hills

desc:
You can see the Tower Hills in the east and south, growing apart from the main
range which is in the west. But the hills don't seem to continue long in the
east. In the west, the hills go on until they reach the main hill range. The
ground here is hard and rocky, and only a few bushes grow.
name: Steep Hillside

desc:
Small hills, covered with dry grass and bushes, stretch around the area, slowly
declining towards the plains westwards. Several great and majestic oaks grew
here for years, but only rotten logs and branches remain. A long ridge of steep
hills rises to the south, blocking the view.
name: Small Hills

desc:
The narrow path abruptly ends in a dead-end where two hill ridges meet. The thin
and sandy soil does not appear to be rich. Only a few bushes manage to get
enough water to survive in this arid place. Underlying rocks are peeking through
the sand in several places.
name: The Small Path

desc:
The path winds between hills, getting more narrow southwards. The dark dirt off
the trail is wet and surprisingly barren. But looking closer, one may notice a
lot of animal tracks.
name: The Small Path

desc:
A thick layer of earth covers the slopes of the narrow valley, feeding tall,
green bushes and lush grass. The hillside, slowly climbing westwards, becomes
more barren with only a few low bushes growing on the small, rocky plateaus. The
path winds in a north-south direction, strictly following a winding landscape.
name: The Small Path

desc:
The trail continues here, going through a grove of ancient elm trees and bending
southwards. Water is bubbling out of the ground below a pile of small rocks and
ends up in a small hollow, forming a lake. The trees disappear as a path enters
the long, narrow valley between two steep ridges.
name: The Small Path

desc:
The wide path travels from west to east, joining the road to the Grey Havens in
a small junction northwards. The soil covering the trail is mud-like, but the
layer of dirt covering the stony base is quite thin. The southern edge of the
path slowly slopes towards the large, grass-covered hill. A group of tall,
slender trees grows eastwards, forming a small, shadowy grove.
name: The Small Path

desc:
Flowers of different colours spot these plains, but dark, green grass is
dominant and covers all the landscape nearby. The gentle wind carries a sweet
scent in the air, attracting bees and other insects. Looking west, there seems
to be a large forest many miles away.
name: Small Hills

desc:
Large patches of healthy, green grass are spread about the vast and rather damp
grasslands. The earth slowly rises towards the rocky incline northwards, but
going southwards, rich and fertile plains are spread as far as one can see. The
wind whistles around, waving the grass.
name: The Small Path

desc:
A nice, green garden is before you. There are all sorts of vegetables and some
fruits growing here. You walk carefully between rows of plants on a stone- paved
path.
name: Vegetable Garden

desc:
This is a very clean and tidy place. A fireplace can be seen in the west wall.
There are some chairs and a big couch, where you could rest, near the fireplace.
Some clothes can be observed on a hanger in the south corner. You feel sure that
the owners are very friendly people who wouldn't mind your resting here for a
while.
name: Elven Farm House

desc:
Between a few trees, more or less straight rows of bushes covered with flowers
are growing. Soft grass covers the ground under the bushes. A soft humming is
coming from the bees which are busy flying from flower to flower. To the south,
you can see a bend in the road and to the north, a glimpse of a beehive.
name: Some Trees

desc:
Before a small clearing in front of the beehive is a large bed of flowers. Like
the bushes, the flowers grow in straight rows and different kinds of flowers
grow in separated parts of the flowerbed. Bees are flying around collecting
nectar from the flowers. Towards the east the ground becomes muddy. Behind the
beehive and west of it, are the flat plains.
name: In Front of a Giant Beehive

desc:
Bees are very busy storing the collected honey. They hum angrily as they are
disturbed. Deep inside the beehive the queen bee is resting. Her workers are
always trying to keep her fed. The honey is stored in large honeycombs to be
used during winter. Every single honeycomb is placed in a wooden frame. Each
frame has its it own insertion, which makes it easy for the beekeeper to get the
honeycombs out of the beehive.
name: Giant Beehive

desc:
The ground is very muddy here and the strange smell becomes stronger near an
extremely muddy area. This area looks as if it can give way any second. The
water coming from the south vanishes into the mud. North and east are empty
plains and west is an area full of life.
name: Knee-deep Mud

desc:
Up from the earth comes a clear stream of water. A small brook flows eastwards
through empty plains towards a destination unknown. The rest of the water runs
northwards into a dark hole. The ground is rather muddy here and a strange smell
lies in the air. The area in north looks unnatural and even more muddy.
name: Small Spring

desc:
Rich, green grass covers the ground under a large group of trees. One can almost
say that it is a small, light forest. The forest is surrounded by small bushes
which grow thinner towards the north. West and south is the road. A wooden sign
is nailed to a tree here.
name: Trees

desc:
You are standing in the entrance hall of an ancient elven stronghold. You can
see paintings with battle scenes on the wall. Weapons are displayed on the walls
and armour is standing in the corners of the room. You can leave up to the
castle walls.
name: Elven Stronghold

desc:
Hmm...., this path seems to strangely end here all of a sudden. Maybe, those
building this path ran out of time or motivation behind it. You see some big
boulders to the south and a steep rock-face towards north. To the west, you
observe that someone has dumped large blocks of iron not needed anywhere else.
The rain and snow have rusted most of it but you still cannot see what lies
beyond it, but looking up westwards, you see huge walls like those of a castle.
name: Path between the Hills

desc:
You are on a quite broad path between high hills. There is nothing much to
observe here except that this path looks like its leading to something not many
people are supposed to know about. The path is smooth and wide and you have no
trouble walking on it.
name: Path between the Hills

desc:
This is a very small cave in the Tower Hills surrounding the valley. Here, a
spring can be seen in the north-east part of the cave. The small stream coming
from the spring moves towards the south end of the cave. Following the stream
north is impossible, as the cave becomes too small for that. The walls of the
cave are covered with elven drawings. To the north, you see a path leading
somewhere. It does not seem very used but someone has been keeping it clear of
weeds and bushes.
name: Cave in the Tower Hills

desc:
This cave doesn't lead far into the Tower Hills. There is a spring coming from
the north. A small stream coming from the spring runs west and vanishes into a
crack. The crack is too narrow to crawl in and the only way out of the caves is
the entrance to the south. The spring is used to supply the elves with water.
Some devices are built here for filling buckets easily.
name: Cave in the Tower Hills

desc:
This is a very small cave in the Tower Hills surrounding the valley. Here a
spring can be seen in the north-east part of the cave. The small stream coming
from the spring vanishes in the west end of the cave. Following the stream is
impossible, the cave becomes too small for that. The walls of the cave are
covered with elven pictures.
name: Cave in the Tower Hills

desc:
This is where the residents of this building spend most of their time. The room
is as luxurious as any you have seen. Chairs, tables and couches are placed so
as to allow more than one group to discuss privately, their important events and
plans. Windows open towards east and north letting in fresh vale air.
name: Living Quarters

desc:
The floor of the main hall of this building is entirely covered with marble. On
the eastern and western side of the hall you can each see four impressive
statues. The statues resemble the eight Aratar: Manwe and Varda, Ulmo, Yavanna
and Aule, Mandos, Nienna, and Orome. The statues glow with a pure, white light
and were made with fascinating skill. There are doors leading into other rooms
of the building like the kitchen and the living rooms.
name: Main Hall

desc:
As you look around this room, you are dazzled by the beauty of every item you
see here. The walls are made of marble just like the rest of the place. There is
a large bed near the south wall covered with silken sheets. There are some
cushioned arm-chairs neatly arranged around a table and a candelabrum in the
centre of the table. The floor is covered with gorgeous rugs which are very
comforting to your feet.
name: Large Bedroom

desc:
By far the most impressive kitchen you have seen in a long time. There is plenty
of room to walk between the long kitchen tables and counters. There is a window
to the west and a flue going up. The smell of food is pleasant but not strong.
You can hear someone talking about farming matters nearby.
name: Kitchen

desc:
Here is the centre of this hidden elven valley. There are elven looking houses
spread all over the valley. It is quiet and peaceful here and the elves are
friendly toward friendly looking strangers. It seems that they don't care too
much that one might give away the location of their hidden valley and why should
they? The days of Morgoth are long gone. To the north is a small cave and to the
west is a marvellous looking marble building. The valley is surrounded by the
sky-high Tower Hills and there is no way out except through the passage to the
south.
name: Elven Valley

desc:
A passage begins here leading east inside the Tower Hills. In the north there is
a small valley shining with a bright, white light. There must be some magic
hidden in the valley. The valley winds itself between the Tower Hills that makes
it impossible to look far inside the valley and discover what is hiding there.
name: Hidden Passage

desc:
From this cave-hall inside the Tower Hills there is a narrow passage leading
west into darkness. The cave-hall was carved long ago into the rock to build a
guard post to protect the passage. In the north part of the cave-hall is a small
resting-area for the guards who keep the watch. Next to it is a small prison
cell. East of the cave-hall, a tunnel leads up to the surface. This place is
likely to be under guard at all times, so be careful.
name: Hidden Passage

desc:
A small creek flows here from north towards south-east and vanishes into the
hills somewhere. The water is clear and drinkable. Channels have been dug to use
the water for the farmlands in the valley in the west. You can also see several
large pits lined with stones, probably for the use of animals.
name: At a Small Creek

desc:
The terrain on top of the Tower Hills is rough and rocky. There are no paths
anymore which would allow riding on horseback in this terrain. There isn't any
vegetation growing here but a cold breeze is constantly blowing over the rocks
and in every crevice. Between the Tower Hills, there is a small valley.
name: Tower Hills

desc:
The Tower Hills continue in the east. In the north, the path slopes down into a
range of small hills which continue until they meet the main hill range in the
far west. The ground here is rough and hard. Small, dry bushes grow here which
are the only signs of life.
name: Tower Hills

desc:
The plains are more barren here than in the surrounding area, only small patches
of low grass dot the dry, waterless soil. Even trees find it hard to grow in
these arid lands. A dusty path bends here, going towards the ridge of hills to
the west and a large brush-covered field northwards.
name: Small Hills

desc:
High grass and various flowers completely obscure the ground on these fertile
grasslands. Several blackberry bushes are spread along the tiny trail running
through the plains. A small hill rises in elevation to the west. Farther east
the soil becomes drier and the vegetation is less dense.
name: Small Hills

desc:
The landscape is slowly rising towards the south-west and it isn't as flat as it
is in the north-east. Small hills alternate with gentle slopes and rich, green
but flat valleys. The valleys are totally covered with high vegetation. A couple
of miles in the south the Tower Hills are rise up high in the sky.
name: In Front of the Tower Hills

desc:
The main part of the small plateau is covered by a large bird's nest. It is
about three feet in diameter and made out of hundreds or thousands of bush-
branches. The edge is covered with bird droppings. Bones and furs of small
animals like rabbits are scattered all over the plateau.
name: Hawk's Nest

desc:
The landscape changes here. In the north, you can see a large hill where bushes
grow. Towards the west, you can see the beginning of the tower hills. A large
number of bushes are growing here. The grass here has a huge variety of colours
and lengths, and the whole place is very pleasant looking.
name: Grasslands

desc:
This hill is a bit larger than the others on the rolling plains. It offers an
excellent view over the plains. To the south-west are the Tower Hills and to the
east are the Far Downs. To the north the road to the Grey Havens is a faint line
against the horizon. In the south and south east the plains continue.
name: Large Hill

desc:
Up and down small hills it goes here. The hills are covered with thick, rich
grass. Bushes and trees grow on the hills and in the valleys between the hills.
Mole-holes are under the bushes and broken off leaves indicate the presence of
larger animals. To the south the steppe begins and to the east is a large hill
from where one can get a splendid view over the plains.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
An endless looking flat plain stretches south until it reaches the Tower Hills.
North of the rocky land is a rolling plain. The only thing growing on the rocky
land is grass. From the tracks on the ground it is possible to judge that not
many living things travel through the rocky land.
name: Rocky Land

desc:
The land here is covered with soft, lush green grass which comes up to your
ankles in height in most places. The plains continue to the east and to the
south. The Tower Hills rise in the west and north and obstruct passage in these
directions. Small, furry little animals rush by you in search of food and
sometimes trying to get away from a predator.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The landscape undergoes a swift change here. To the south small hills rise,
covered with dense vegetation. In the west, the land gets rough as the plains
contain small hillocks and big rocks leading towards the tower hills. In the
east, you can see a small forest. Tulips and other bulbous flowers grow here. A
few rabbit holes can be seen and there are tracks of foxes near the holes. The
rough plains to the north appear hard to pass through, but the way is clear in
all other directions.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
The brushwood is almost a small forest here consisting only of deciduous trees.
Most of the trees are maple trees and occasionally there is a beech growing
between them. The ground is covered with rich grass and the bushes are standing
close together. A squirrel lair is built in an old tree stump.
name: Brushwood

desc:
A dense hedge out of head-high bushes marks the eastern edge of the brushwood.
Bird nests of small birds are built in the higher bushes and in the branches of
maple trees. In the tree-trunk of one of the larger trees there is a woodpecker
nest. Knee-high above the ground the bark of the trees shows signs of boar
tusks.
name: Brushwood

desc:
In the south, a group of very thorny bushes is growing. To the west, is a large
hill and in the east, a lone tree. Many animal tracks are leading from a
brushwood in the north past the large hill and then along the bushes to the lone
tree. The ground is covered with soft, green grass. Parts of the grass are
trampled into the ground by the hoofed animals which pass here.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
Oops, these bushes are really spiky!!! You are spiked with thorns from head to
toe. The grass is shrivelled and you can see NO signs of animals here. The long
thorns make your progress very difficult and thinking of the other unpleasant
things that are maybe here gives you an urgent desire to leave this place head
over heels.
name: Thorny Bushes

desc:
An area of thorny bushes is in the west. In the east and the south, a huge rock
rises up towards the sky. In the north, you can see a large tree standing in a
sea of grass. The way east appears very difficult.
name: Grassy Plain

desc:
The grass grows knee-high here, caressing your legs when a soft breeze blows.
The vegetation grows dense in the east, and looking far in the east, you see a
dark grove of tall trees. To the west and north, you can see pleasant, lush
green grassy plains.
name: Tall Grass

desc:
The vegetation is quite dense here and grows a lot denser towards east, where
you can see a dark grove of tall trees that look gloomy. In the west, you can
see a field with tall, green grass growing in it. The passage north is blocked
by strange, poisonous looking weeds and bushes. To the south, you see grassy
plains stretching endlessly leading nowhere.
name: Dense Vegetation

desc:
You are standing in front of a large oak tree. On the ground, you can see many
animal tracks leading down to a water-hole. The water-hole is obviously the
explanation for the many animals coming here. A rope ladder is leading up the
tree to a tree house. Who lives in this forsaken area? The large shadow of the
tree looks inviting to take a rest.
name: Large Tree

desc:
The grassland stretches in every direction mile after mile. Occasionally, you
can see groups of bushes and sometimes you can even get a glimpse of a tree or
two. The beds of flowers here have an almost hypnotic fragrance and you have a
sudden urge to sink into one of these beds and lie down and drift away on a
sparkling cloud into the land of dreams.
name: Grasslands

desc:
In the north, you can barely see the road to Grey Havens. Small rabbits are
hopping through the grass. The innocent smell of the beautiful flowers makes you
relax. A soft breeze is blowing over the grassy ocean perfectly suiting this
peaceful area.
name: Grasslands

desc:
The ground here is a little rough but it appears to get rougher in the east. To
the south, the vegetation becomes quite dense and you snort as a whiff of foul
odour comes from the south... no use going that way. In the west, the plains
become even and covered with soft, lush green grass. The road is to the north.
name: Rough Field

desc:
This field is certainly rough and hard to walk on. Stones, large and small,
half-hidden in the tall and thick grass make every step a torture for your feet.
To the north, you can see the road to the Grey Havens, and to the south, you can
see a grove of tall trees but it is too dense for you to be able to get through.
name: Rough Field

desc:
The grassland is very dry here, perhaps some local water supply problem. A hot
wind is blowing tumbleweed over the ground and you can see a few dried out
bushes. In the more sandy areas you can see the tracks of small animals, maybe
rabbits or snakes?
name: Grasslands

desc:
The grassland ends to the north, marked by the road to the Grey Havens. The
grassland spreads out like an endless meadow full of flowers. The grass is more
than knee-high, and traces of many small animals can easily be discerned. To the
east is a small path stamped through the grass, clearly produced by someone in
boots, but the tracks appear to be rather old.
name: Grasslands

desc:
The grassland runs along the Road to the Grey Havens. Tall, shoulder-high bushes
are growing here forming a hard-to-pass barrier in the south. You can see tracks
of larger animals like boars and elks. There are for sure also predators here
looking for prey.
name: Grasslands

desc:
The grassland stretches west far beyond the horizon, maybe it covers even all
the way to the Grey Havens. A small snake is sneaking through the grass trying
to catch a rabbit or a mouse. You can hear the deep rutting call of a large elk
trying to impress a female elk.
name: Grasslands

desc:
You are standing in a clearing in the midst of the grassland. An ideal place to
rest. Small animals are bouncing around. You feel safe from predators. You can't
be sure where you are but you can see a narrow path leading north- south.
Following the path might lead you back to the road or maybe to a populated area.
name: Clearing

desc:
A group of twelve stout oak trees is growing here in a perfect circle. Inside of
the circle is a small, chest high, grassy hill. This ancient relic is surrounded
by endless miles of the savanna. To the north you can see the faint line of the
road to the Grey Havens against the horizon.
name: Stand of Oak Trees

desc:
Looking south and east you see the endless grassland going on and on towards the
horizon. A soft breeze blows over the grassland from the south. A couple of
animals' tracks are leading west. Hmm, did you just see a shadow vanishing
behind the bush? Maybe someone is here watching you!
name: Grasslands

desc:
A narrow path leads north-south through the grassland. As your eyes follow the
path southwards you can see a large tree. You also notice animal tracks leading
towards that direction. Maybe something interesting is there? To the west the
vegetation becomes denser and east you can see a couple of trees.
name: Grasslands

desc:
A small stream is coming from the north slowing down its pace towards the south
as it is slowly widening. The tunnel walls are covered with mud and one can not
say for sure if this tunnel was built by some living being or dug in the ground
by the rushing water. A faint light is coming from the south and replacing the
dim light of the fungi.
name: Muddy Tunnel

desc:
This side part of the tunnel is leading north into a rotten room. The main part
of the tunnel is to the west. The ground is covered with disgusting looking
slime and a few bones are lying around. Many have entered here but only a few
have made it out again alive, but the reason for the poisonous stench is not
apparent here.
name: Dark Tunnel

desc:
A series of small caverns that are connected through a narrow tunnel is leading
from the north towards the east. The tunnel is partly collapsed so that the only
way through it is to climb or crawl over piles of muddy earth. A dim light is
radiating from fungi that grow all over here.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel is leading in a sharp bend from the west to the south. In the east
wall is a large opening. What is behind the opening is covered in darkness. The
only hint of what might be there is a poisonous stench coming from the east. Out
of a crack in the ground, a stream of water is coming which rushes towards the
south.
name: Muddy Tunnel

desc:
Old and rotten wooden shores are stabilizing the muddy cavern. The cavern is the
entrance to a tunnel leading south. A soft but cold draught is coming out of the
tunnel. A small water-stream is coming from above rushing past the cavern and
then vanishes in a crack. The cavern is barely lit by a pale light that comes
from the south.
name: Muddy Cavern

desc:
The cavern originally was a natural one but someone has widened it into a large
room. It looks like it once was a smuggler hideout. Now, some of the most
revolting living things inhabit this cavern. The ground has a strange green
colour and doesn't look all that solid. The poisonous stench is coming up from
the ground and the room is filled with piles of bones and skulls.
name: Horrible Cavern

desc:
Well-tended looking bushes are growing in straight rows, running in a north-
south direction here. The bushes are waist-high and covered with thousands of
flowers. From the north the soft babbling of a brook is coming. In the west is a
large group of trees. East and north-east are empty plains.
name: Bushes

desc:
Between ancient trees, a lot of undergrowth is growing, and this makes moving in
any direction very difficult. In the north, you see a high, wooden fence covered
in ivy. The tops of large tents are visible over the fence. To the south you see
the road, while a path leads through the undergrowth in the west. To your east a
dense grove stands.
name: Dense Vegetation

desc:
The grove is not very dense here. Whichever direction you move in, you feel as
if you are walking on a path. The ground is rough though, and you have to look
down occasionally to avoid stumbling over some root or bush. The grove seems to
be getting denser towards east and to the south. You can see the forest growing
sparser in the west.
name: Dense Grove

desc:
In front of the gate to north leading inside an elven camp is a small clearing.
Elven guards are guarding the gate from evil beings. The camp is surrounded by a
wood-made fence that prevents wild animals from entering the camp. The gate is
secured with a primitive bolt. Behind the gate is a fireplace, a couple of tents
and a paddock.
name: Entrance to the Elven Hunting Camp

desc:
There is a large, stone-made fireplace here. Next to the fireplace is a grill.
The elves use this fireplace to roast meat for their meals. A delicious aroma is
coming from the tent in the north, like there is a kitchen there. A bit west of
the fireplace is a pond and to the east is a group of tents.
name: Fireplace

desc:
There is a small pond next to the fence surrounding the camp. The pond is
supplied with water by a small brook coming from the west. A couple of buckets
are placed near the pond. To the west there is a fence and behind the gate a
small, juicy meadow. The meadow is also surrounded by a wooden fence. To the
north is the entrance of a tent.
name: Small Pond

desc:
In the smoking chamber the elves smoke the meat they get from their hunts to
keep it edible over a long period of time. They also store the already smoked
meat in here to keep it dry. On the side of the chamber there is wood piled up
that is used for smoking the meat. You can also see a big flue for directing the
smoke into the open air.
name: Smoking Chamber

desc:
Inside this large tent there is the kitchen of the camp. In here the elven cook
boils the more delicate food. A large cooker is standing in the north and at the
sides of the tent are cupboards. Above the cooker is a flue. Kitchen utensils
are spread all over the kitchen table and some large pots and saucepans are
placed in a corner of the tent.
name: Cooking Tent

desc:
At the east and west side of the tent are elven beds. The pillows and the quilt
covers are made out of soft but robust looking green cloth. In the north part of
the tent stands a large wardrobe. The floor is covered with a brightly coloured
carpet. One can see flowery patterns on the carpet.
name: Sleeping Tent

desc:
This simple tent is built out of a couple of wooden poles and a thick canopy
made out of water-repellent cloth. The tent's purpose is to offer a meeting
place sheltered from rain and snow or other forms of bad weather. The tent is
also used for taking meals. A table with nicely placed dishes on it is here.
name: Common Tent

desc:
On the west side of the tent there are two elven beds. The beds are decorated
with beautiful ornaments. The bedding is made out of yellow silk. This tent
belongs to the more important elves of the camp. In the south is a small
facility for the morning washing. In the east part of the tent are big chests
containing different sets of clothes for different occasions.
name: Sleeping Tent

desc:
The elves of this elven camp keep their horses in this paddock. The ground is
covered with a juicy meadow. A small brook is entering the meadow from the west
and leaving it to the east. To the north there is smoke coming up out of the
earth. Behind the wooden fence are green plains filled with bushes.
name: Paddock

desc:
Inside the storage tent there are hunting weapons and other hunting utensils.
stored. Spare parts for the tents are stacked in one corner and horse brass is
lying on the floor in the west part of the tent. The cook stores the food he
needs for cooking in this tent. You smell smoke coming up from a trapdoor.
name: Storage Tent

desc:
This is the western end of the village. To the south can be heard the pleasant
tinkling of many elven voices raised in a song. The houses, like the rest of
this elven village, are built of a sturdy but amazingly light material which
blends in with the local flora and fauna.
name: Elven Village

desc:
A sign hanging here draws your attention. Despite the sign, you get the feeling
that you are at home here. This place appears to be full of people indulging in
merry singing and laughter. Many shades of grey and brown fight for dominance
here, but this enhances the natural wooden colour of the ambience. The tables
appear to have been cut out of trees, parts of which still form the walls of
this bar.
name: The Laughing Elf

desc:
The inn is lit by lanterns on the walls and furnished with a few round tables
and some chairs. From the north, you can hear voices and laughter from the
people in the bar. Towards east, there is a large mirror. The whole place is
well kept and feels comfortable, just like most structures in this village.
name: A Small, Cosy Inn

desc:
As you enter this room, you find yourself in a large room with beds and couches
neatly arranged near the walls. The meagre furnishings in the room illustrate
the hard and simple lifestyle of the guards. There are lanterns hung on the
walls at regular spacing between beds. Northwards are what look like bathing
rooms. Some beds have small tables near them with a few books stacked up.
name: Warrior Barracks

desc:
The bushes and small trees become very dense here and making progress here is
very difficult. The undergrowth is not green as the rest of the rich grassland
but more brown and the grass on the ground is very dry. The animals living in
this area normally avoid this unfriendly place.
name: Undergrowth

desc:
Just as you expected, this is a very clean barn unlike some others you may have
seen before. There are cows and pigs in different parts of the barn, and a whole
section has been fenced in for the fowl. Different farming implements can be
seen lying around.
name: Barn

desc:
In the north you can barely see the road to the Grey Havens. You are standing in
an endless-looking ocean of grass. The colour of the grass changes between
bright green and the brown-yellow of dried out grass. Further in the south the
vegetation becomes more dense. In the east, the fields appear a bit rough.
name: Grasslands

desc:
From the cliff it is possible to get a superb view over the plain that lies
north-east of the Tower Hills. The grassy plain stretches north until it reaches
the road to the Grey Havens. The cliff itself goes suddenly steep down several
hundred feet with no way to climb down.
name: Cliff in the Tower Hills

desc:
The grasslands stretch far to the north and south. In the west, you can see a
cliff made out of sandstone which rises a little above the ground. Far in the
west, you can see the tower hills. Towards the east, you can see a quite large
area where the grass grows much taller than here. Beyond the grass, in the east,
you can barely see a dense grove of trees which stretch for long near the
horizon.
name: Grasslands

desc:
In the midst of the grass plains, a solid sandstone rock is rising up in the
sky. The western parts of the sandstone hillock are corroded and rocks have
fallen down which makes it impossible to climb up the hillock. The top of the
hillock also looks broken up and rocks are lying around. Small strips of brown
bear fur hang from a sharp edge of a rock.
name: Sandstone Hill

desc:
You are walking through an endless grassland which stretches into all
directions. You have the feeling of being hopelessly lost. Head high bushes and
trees block your view and you just can't figure out where you are. Besides being
lost, you begin to like the grassland. The rich vegetation and the many animals
make the grassland a superb hunting ground. But maybe some others have
discovered this too ...
name: Grasslands

desc:
The entrance of this small niche is partly covered with rocks. The niche is well
sheltered from the weather. Bones and furs of smaller animals like rabbits are
lying around and in the back of the niche a set of antlers which once belonged
to a young, male elk are resting.
name: Under a Ledge

desc:
A small opening is there in the roof which may keep the room lit during the day.
A tunnel leads north under the grassy plains. Out of the tunnel, a small stream
of water is coming. The stream leads up a little before vanishing in the ground.
The tunnel's ground level appears to be about the same depth as the water level
in this area.
name: Muddy Hole

desc:
It is in this spot that animals from all over the plains congregate, seeking to
quench their thirst. The water-hole is a large brown pond, fed by an under-
water supply. No fish swim here and the land surrounding the hole is sandy and
covered with tracks.
name: Water-hole

desc:
You are inside a ranger's home. Large windows give you an overview over the
whole savanna. The furniture in this room is plain and simple. You can only see
a bed, a table and a chair. The ranger who lives here is surely a true guardian
of the savanna and a defender of all good.
name: Ranger's Home in a Large Tree

desc:
The lush grass that surrounds this place grows even taller and thicker here,
hiding the muddy soil that lies beneath. The dense vegetation provides ample
footing and there is no threat of sinking into the muck. The water appears to be
rising out of the ground itself, as there is no sign of a stream. Further to the
south, the grass is replaced by reeds.
name: Damp Lowlands

desc:
Short, thick grass covers most of the land, though there are many small stony
patches and large rocks punctuating the greenery. The land rises and dips in
small waves, gradually dropping as it flows toward the east. Hundreds of yellow
flowers dot the grass and add variety to the scenery. A low wall of natural rock
rises to the west, while the smell of rotting vegetation drifts in from the
south.
name: Edge of a Lush Valley

desc:
A mixture of tall sedge grass and broad reeds dominate this area. The land is
submerged in most spots by an inch or two of standing water, and the soil below
is thick mud. Mats of crushed and dead vegetation allow adequate footing with no
fear of sinking. At most times, there is no sound but the whispering breeze and
the dull hum of invisible insects.
name: Flooded Lowlands

desc:
Short, thick-bladed grass struggles to survive in this drenched area, vying for
space with tall reeds and cattails. Every depression in the ground is filled
with brown, silt-laden water. A few stunted trees cling to life, but many more
have fallen and their rotting trunks sport a colourful array of fungi. A short
cliff rises over the area to the west.
name: Flooded Lowlands

desc:
Many trees once grew here, but most have succombed to the ravages of flooding.
While some twisted willow and cypress trees still rise above the land, many
others lie rotting, sporting dozens of species of puffballs, bracket fungi, and
mushrooms. The ground is entirely saturated, and only a few winding paths of
thick grass and reeds allows any passage here. A short cliff rises to the west.
name: Thick Swamp

desc:
Large stretches of shallow, stinking water dominate the area, interspersed with
paths and islands of thick, clinging mud. Only those plants able to survive the
perpetual flooding of this land grow here, but they are thriving in the dense,
nutrient-rich soil. Hundreds of wildflowers dot the otherwise drab landscape.
The swamp grows deeper and impassable to the south and east.
name: Deep Swamp

desc:
The plateau ends here in a rounded corner. Vertical cliffs fall away to the
east, where a lower plateau covered in thick grass can be seen, and to the
south, where a road runs through a very low set of hills. The ground has been
smoothed by ages of wind and rain, yet it is still very uneven in many places.
name: Edge of a Barren Plateau

desc:
This plateau extends for a couple of miles in every direction but to the south.
There, it drops off in a sheer cliff of more than four hundred feet. From the
edge it is possible to make out a road running east and west near the cliff's
base, but a line of trees beside the road make it difficult to observe. Beyond
the road is a shorter set of hills.
name: Plateau Overlooking a Road

desc:
The plateau ends abruptly in a rounded corner here, with steep cliffs dropping
off to both the south and west. The ground is level and carpeted with a dense
layer of short, dark-green grass. This spot provides an excellent view of the
road that runs near the base of the plateau, and from trampled patches in the
grass and a small lean-to built back from the edge, out of sight of the road, it
may often be used for exactly that purpose.
name: Concealed Observation Post upon a Plateau

desc:
This hill is a smaller cousin of the Tower Hills that rise to the north. Its
slopes are gentle and easy to navigate. Thin grass reaches a foot or so in
height, while white- and yellow-petaled eyebright and pearlwort rise above the
grass on slender stems, adding variety to the terrain. The hills grow taller and
rockier to the west.
name: Top of a Low Hill

desc:
Dozens of low rocky peaks thrust skyward, none of them of particularly large or
tall. Jumbled boulders and stones lie strewn about everywhere, requiring some
caution while moving. Many of the rocks are dotted with pale lichen, while
others are coated with thick layers of bright green moss. Beyond and below the
boulders and rocks to the north, a road winds its way through the valleys
in-between the hills.
name: Low Hills

desc:
These rocky humps are barely tall enough to be called hills, though they are
still rugged enough to slow travel. The hills are covered in equal parts of
short, sturdy grass and bare brown rock. A narrow but deep crevice runs between
the hills, severing the area from another set of hills just beyond and creating
an isolated dead-end within the rocky terrain.
name: Low Hills

desc:
This hillside dips to the north and east, where a road winds its way through a
valley. It is low and not very steep, allowing a fair accumulation of soil and
plantlife. Coarse grass, low and broad-bladed, dominates the area, fighting for
dominance with thistles and other weeds. Dandelions grow rampant among the other
plants, dotting the hillside with specks of yellow.
name: Low Hillside

desc:
The road ascends a low, grassy hilltop, avoiding the more rocky and broken land
to either side. The grass is thick and healthy, and has started to encroach on
the road, threatening to overgrow it if not cut back. Taller hills rise to both
the north and south, with the road running in a nearly straight line between
them.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road runs both east and north, bending its way around a tall plateau. The
road shows evidence of having been washed out several times, but it has been
repaired recently and is in good condition. Thin weeds and stunted shrubs grow
on either side, struggling to survive in the sandy soil. Low hills border the
road to the south and west.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
A tall plateau rises to the north, an ominous sentinel standing guard over the
land. The road runs almost due east and west near the base of the plateau. Many
piles of broken rocks line the north edge of the road, and in some places they
extended across and beyond the road, though the surface has now been cleared of
stones.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
A tall plateau stands over the road to the north, while a much smaller hillside
rises to the south. A small line of evergreens grow near the base of the cliff.
They are mostly healthy, although several of them have been damaged or even
completely smashed by falling rocks. A narrow dirt path splits off from the road
and runs south into the hills.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road wraps around the base of a low hill to the southwest, maintaining as
level a course as possible. As it extends further to the west, it runs along the
base of a tall cliff. Thick grass runs along the eastern edge of the road and
covers a series of low rolling hills in that direction, while the western hills
seem to have rockier and less fertile soil.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The earthy path is wide and well-travelled here; a lot of wagon tracks and
footprints mark it. To the west, thick bushes surround the path, which gets
increasingly narrow. Following the path to the east, it leads to the road to the
Grey Havens.
name: Small Dirt Path

desc:
A dirty path runs along the base of the the large hill and widens westwards.
Tiny round stones litter the hillside, giving no place for vegetation to grow.
Five-foot high bushes frame the path, protecting it from direct sunlight.
name: Small Dirt Path

desc:
The tall and dense shrubbery conceals everything behind it, giving a home to
various small animals. Only a few trees managed to get through the thick carpet
of bushes to get to the sunlight. The path bends around the mossy rocks and
large bushes. It continues to the east and west into the distance, but to the
north it seems to connect to a larger road.
name: Small Dirt Path

desc:
A narrow trail of hard-packed earth runs from the north, where it connects to a
larger road, to the south, where it winds its way into a series of hills. The
path seems to be regularly travelled, as the grass that springs up on either
side has not been able to reclaim it. A thinner, less used path extends from
here to the east, creating a second connection to the road.
name: Small Dirt Path

desc:
Actually a very low hilltop, this field peaks near its centre, dropping off
slightly in every direction. Short, thick grass covers the ground, though in
some places where the soil is sandy the grass is replaced by thistles and other
weeds. A few stunted shrubs dot the area, sporting blossoms of pale yellow and
pink. Farther away to the south, an area of densely growing bushes borders the
road.
name: Broad Grassy Field

desc:
Were it not for scattered boulders and upthrusts of stone, this meadow could be
mistaken for a broad, flat plain. The grass grows tall and lush. Narrow animal
trails weave a dizzying network through the vegetation, and there are a number
of signs of grazing. The meadow ends at a low cliff to the east. A flooded area
with sick and dying vegetation lies at its base.
name: Green Meadow

desc:
Thousands of stones of every shape and size litter this meadow, from minute
pebbles barely larger than sand to enormous boulders and upthrusts of brown and
orange rock. Several species of grass have gained purchase between the stones,
from short and thin but sturdy blades to tall, broad blades of emerald green.
The meadow ends abruptly at a cliff that runs along its east and north edges.
name: Rocky Meadow

desc:
Several species of grasses vie with one another for primacy over this area.
Tor-grass and whorl-grass seem to dominate, interspersed with taller oat-grass
and to a lesser extent with thick-stemmed horsetails and white-tipped
cottongrass. The field extends in every direction except to the west, where a
tall plateau rises overhead and casts the field into deep shadows every
afternoon.
name: Fields below a Plateau

desc:
The road extends in a straight line to the west where it ascends a small hill.
To the east, a tall plateau rises over the road, shading it through much of the
morning and forcing it to turn south. A pile of broken rocks lies at the base of
the cliff, broken from its surface by ages of weathering. Some of the stones lie
on the road itself, warning of the ever-present danger of landslides.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
You keep on walking on the road to the Grey Havens. The countryside begins to
change slightly. The vegetation becomes richer and greener than before. North of
you are groups of trees and dense vegetation and to the south an endless savanna
stretches out as far as you can see. The road runs from east to west here.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road goes on from east to west. Its surface is smooth and makes for easy
walking or riding. A few small cracks can be seen in the old stones. Maybe a
mole or a mouse has been trying to dig a home below. It seems time does not stop
even for this ancient road. To the north, dense and thick green vegetation
grows, while the areas south of the road seem to be much drier.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
Dense vegetation is surrounding the path to the east and west. The trees and the
undergrowth to the west are impenetrable. To the north it is possible to enter
the vegetation. The path is leading further north towards the entrance of an
elven hunting camp.
name: Path in the Vegetation

desc:
The Road to the Grey Havens leads straight from the east to the west. North of
the road are trees and north-east is thick undergrowth. To the south is the
savanna. Between the trees north of you, you can see the remains of an old camp:
a cold fireplace roughly built out of a circle of stone and the usual trash that
travellers leave behind.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
You walk on the Road to the Grey Havens. The road is in a good condition here
and flows east-west. The trees to the north slowly vanish, replaced by bushes
and meadows. South of the road is the endless savanna which seems to be typical
for this area.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The Road to the Grey Havens bends north-east here. North of the road are empty
plains. Obviously the plains in the north are not supplied with enough water for
the growth of vegetation. Maybe the underground can't hold the water and it
vanishes in unknown depths? South is an endless looking savanna.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
You are walking on the road to the Grey Havens. The countryside is not very
interesting here. The savannas have faded away and the road is surrounded by
plains that stretch far to the horizon. The road is still in a good condition
and you can see the tracks of horses and horse-wagons on the ground. The road
goes east-west from here.
name: The Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road has run along open plains and fields for that long now. You wonder if
you will ever see some high places around here, as you detect the feet of some
hills or mountains far to the west.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
At this point of the road you begin to see the feet of the tower hills more
clearly. But it is still a few miles before the road enters a valley in the
hills.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road coming from the east makes a sharp bend to the north now. You can see
the Tower hills to the west in a distance. You feel pretty tired after walking
along the road for that long.
name: Bend in the Road

desc:
The road runs north and south at this point. A dusty path splits off to the
west, leading into the tower hills. It is quickly disappears from view as it
bends around boulders and around large patches of shrubs and bushes. Steep
slopes to the east higher into the hills, but those lofty heights are
inaccessible from here.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road bends through a graceful arc, extending onward toward the west and
south. The strong smell of rotting vegetation rises from the east, where an
extensive marsh can be see at the base of a low cliff. A few potholes scar the
surface of the road, but it is otherwise in good condition.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road runs in the shadow of a tall cliff that rises to the south. The road is
badly rutted and in need of repair, but still serviceable enough for now. Tall,
dense bushes border the road to the north, encroaching on it yet not able to
gain any purchase on its hard-packed surface. The bushes extend far to the
north.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
The road makes a sharp bend here, running both north and east. A tall cliff
rises just to the south, leaving the road in shadow much of the time. Trickles
of water glisten on the rocky surface of the cliff, but they vanish immediately
into the soil when they reach the bottom. A narrow dirt path separates from the
road, running west.
name: Road to the Grey Havens

desc:
You are walking through an endless grassland which stretches in all directions.
You have the feeling of being hopelessly lost. Head high bushes and trees block
your view and you just cannot figure out where you are. Besides being lost you
begin to like the grassland. The rich vegetation and the many animals make the
grassland a superb hunting ground.
name: Grasslands

desc:
The path is leading through dense vegetation northwards towards an unknown
destination. West of the path is the road to the Grey Havens. To the south a
barrier of trees is making progress in that direction impossible. To the east
the path is bordered with undergrowth. The path looks like it is not used very
often or the ones using it leave no tracks.
name: Path in the Vegetation

desc:
You have a superb view over the whole valley from here. You can see a few houses
in the valley, small fields and farms. The castle wall itself is a simple stone
wall with embrasures. Archers shooting arrows from here can reach every point in
the valley. It seems to be impossible to take this stronghold.
name: Castle Walls

desc:
The higher the Tower Hills rise in the sky, the thinner the vegetation tends to
become. First the higher bushes vanish and then the lower ones until at the top
only grass is growing along with moss and lichen which cover the rocks. Looking
east, it seems possible to climb to a small plateau. The Tower Hills continue to
rise in the south-west direction.
name: Tower Hills

desc:
A broad fissure cuts several feet into the rock, its upper edge filled with a
dense tangle of weeds and brambles. Below the surface, the fissure gradually
narrows until it vanishes entirely. A layer of soil has accumulated in the base
of the crack. Countless scratches mar is surface, and in one spot the ground has
been hollowed out into a shallow bowl.
name: Narrow Den
